LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT COMCAST CABLE COMMUNICATIONS MANAGEMENT, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT - THE MOTION IS GRANTED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE TOLLING ALLEGATIONS. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO ALLEGE THAT TOLLING EXISTS THROUGH THE DATE OF THE DECERTIFICATION ORDER. SEE HALL V. VARIABLE ANNUITY LIFE INS. CO., 727 F.3D 372, 375 (5TH CIR. 2013); GIOVANNIELLO V. ALM MEDIA, LLC, 726 F.3D 106, 117 (2ND CIR. 2013. THE MOTION IS GRANTED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION TO CURE THE ALLEGATIONS THAT ARE CONTRARY TO BRINKER RESTAURANT CORP. V. SUP. CRT. (2012) 53 CAL.4TH 1004, 1017. THE REMAINDER OF THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE CLASS DECERTIFICATION RULING WAS PROCEDURAL IN NATURE AND DOES NOT HAVE A COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL EFFECT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 (302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT COMCAST CABLE COMMUNICATIONS MANAGEMENT, LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE TOLLING ALLEGATIONS. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO ALLEGE THAT TOLLING EXISTS THROUGH THE DATE OF THE DECERTIFICATION ORDER, DECEMBER 15, 2015. SEE HALL V. VARIABLE ANNUITY LIFE INS. CO., 727 F.3D 372, 375 (5TH CIR. 2013); GIOVANNIELLO V. ALM MEDIA, LLC, 726 F.3D 106, 117 (2ND CIR. 2013). THE REMAINDER OF THE MOTION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT HAS IMPROPERLY RAISED THE COLLATERAL ESTOPPEL ISSUE IN THE REPLY, NOT IN THE INITIAL MOTION. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST H. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-18-2015 Continued To Nov-04-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of The First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-13-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT COMCAST CABLE COMMUNICATIONS MANAGEMENT, LLC'S Motion To Strike Portions Of The First Amended Complaint is granted with 20 days leave to amend as to the tolling allegations. Plaintiff has leave to allege that tolling exists through the date of the decertification order, December 15, 2015. See Hall v. Variable Annuity Life Ins. Co., 727 F.3d 372, 375 (5th Cir. 2013); Giovanniello v. ALM Media, LLC, 726 F.3d 106, 117 (2nd Cir. 2013). The remainder of the motion is denied. Defendant has improperly raised the collateral estoppel issue in the Reply, not in the initial motion. Prevailing party shall prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-15-2015 Continued To Sep-02-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-16-2015 Continued To Jun-03-15 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of The Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-11-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ARLO GARCIA URIARTE OF THE LIBERATION LAW GROUP, P.C., TEL. NO. 695-1000 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. TROY A. VALDEZ OF THE VALDEZ TODD & DOYLE, TEL. NO. 202-5945 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT COMCAST CABLE COMMUNICATIONS MANAGEMENT, LLC's Motion To Strike Portions Of The Complaint - The motion is granted with 20 days leave to amend as to the tolling allegations. Plaintiff has leave to allege that tolling exists through the date of the decertification order. See Hall v. Variable Annuity Life Ins. Co., 727 F.3d 372, 375 (5th Cir. 2013); Giovanniello v. ALM Media, LLC, 726 F.3d 106, 117 (2nd Cir. 2013. The motion is granted with 20 days leave to amend as to the third cause of action to cure the allegations that are contrary to Brinker Restaurant Corp. v. Sup. Crt. (2012) 53 Cal.4th 1004, 1017. The remainder of the motion is denied. The class decertification ruling was procedural in nature and does not have a collateral estoppel effect. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
